comments.author: @giaole5532
comments.text:
Thanks for wonderful video and love for my hometown. (From a Viet living in us. )

People misunderstood we were part of Chinese ancients but we were not. Viet is the name of our DNA in general. Vietnam means a group of Viet people living in the South (next to South China Sea and rivers like Mekong Delta) 

We have a total different look to Chinese. And thousands of years fighting with China near the border to protect our lands.

comments.author: @KevinHensley-w1j3t
comments.text: One of these clips is from Laos 🇱🇦, not Vietnam 🇻🇳. Their hats are different

comments.author: @MirSalar-p4y
comments.text: I spent years trying to understand manifesting, but it always felt like I was missing something. Eventually, I came across Vera Hart’s archetype quiz and decided to try it. My result was surprisingly accurate and helped me understand which approach actually suits me. Definitely worth trying if you’ve been feeling stuck too. It only takes a few minutes ❤️

comments.author: @yanyan4323
comments.text:
I love people, traditions, nature/environment of Vietnam. I also love their values. 
The people is very simple which I love. 
Most especially Vietnam is very much affordable in everything compare to other countries in asia.
Very peaceful to live.
Vietnam is almost 30years in war and now they are successful compare to other country near them.
Salute to all government in Vietnam...
Salute to all Vietnamese.... Keep it up...

comments.text: I was there(13 of us from the Philippines) just this May 9-14, 2026, Danang.. We went to Bana Hills, watched the dragon fire night, Hue, marble mountain, hoi an, the boat ride, and enjoyed their food, market stuffs, etc.. We are planning to go North  or South next time.. Exciting🎉🎉🎉

comments.author: @russelltatro2835
comments.text: I see no garbage, nice clean, and people who respect their country. So nice to see. I have always wanted to visit their. I grew up in the days of the Vietnam War. I'm not sure why I've been drawn to Vietnam since I was young. I just love the people, and their country is absolutely beautiful.

comments.author: @HiddenEarthTV-i1b
comments.text: I love how you captured both sides of Vietnam—the chaotic energy of the cities and the absolute serenity of the riverside communities. It’s truly one of the most surprising and beautiful places in Asia. Stunning cinematography! 🇻🇳✨

replies.author: @BestTravel2
replies.text: So glad you enjoyed the video and saw both sides of Vietnam! It really is a surprising and beautiful place.

comments.author: @EpicEarthYTB
comments.text: I am deeply moved by the strong sense of community and family values shown here. The vibrant street food scene and the morning rituals in the parks offer such a fascinating glimpse into the soul of daily life in Vietnam.

replies.author: @BestTravel2
replies.text: I'm so glad the video conveyed that strong sense of community and the beauty of the daily rituals. It truly is a remarkable aspect of life here.
